notes:
Synapse Agent Protocol (SAP) integration sprint. SAP is a fully on-chain identity, discovery, reputation, and payment layer for AI agents — running on Solana mainnet (Program: SAPpUhsWLJG1FfkGRcXagEDMrMsWGjbky7AyhGpFETZ). Key insight: SAP is chain-agnostic as an *identity* layer — Invoica stays on Base/Polygon/Arbitrum/Solana for settlements, but registers itself on SAP so any agent using SAP discovery can find and call Invoica. Direct product fit: SAP has native x402 escrow mechanics (X402Registry + EscrowModule) that maps exactly to Invoica's settlement flow. SAP also has a Reputation/Feedback system that complements Helixa (EVM) on the Solana side.

DOCS REFS (read before coding):
- SAP SDK: https://github.com/OOBE-PROTOCOL/synapse-sap-sdk/tree/main/docs
- Client bridge: https://github.com/OOBE-PROTOCOL/synapse-client-sdk/tree/main/docs_md/05_SAP.md
- Skills ref: https://synapse.oobeprotocol.ai/skills.md

KEY TECHNICAL FACTS:
- Program ID: SAPpUhsWLJG1FfkGRcXagEDMrMsWGjbky7AyhGpFETZ (Solana mainnet)
- Global Registry PDA: 9odFrYBBZq6UQC6aGyzMPNXWJQn55kMtfigzhLg6S6L5
- SAP requires a Solana wallet for agent registration (PDA signing)
- Wallet to use: SellerBot-3 (26z3UHjGbF2LKbgS2r34BSzBH3DBBoLofF1c2EvaEwWQ) — already has SOL, keypair at x402-test/wallets/seller-keypair.json
- SAP registration cost: ~0.002 SOL rent for AgentAccount PDA
- Package: @oobe-protocol-labs/synapse-sap-sdk (also install @coral-xyz/anchor @solana/web3.js)
- NOTE: Invoica's existing Solana code uses raw JSON-RPC (no @solana/web3.js) — SAP integration adds these deps to backend only
- OOBE API key needed for Synapse Gateway RPC (env: OOBE_API_KEY). Alternatively use public Solana mainnet RPC for SAP reads (https://api.mainnet-beta.solana.com).

tasks.description: Install SAP dependencies, create sap-client singleton, write agent registration script, add env vars
Add SAP discovery API endpoints — let Invoica API users query the SAP agent network and surface Invoica's own SAP profile
tasks.context:
Goal: expose SAP discovery through Invoica's API. Developers using Invoica can query the broader SAP agent network to find other agents by capability, and can see Invoica's own on-chain identity.

FILE: backend/src/routes/sap.ts (NEW, max 150 lines)
  Import getSapClient from '../lib/sap-client'.

  Routes to implement:

  GET /v1/sap/network
    → Returns SAP global registry stats: totalAgents, activeAgents, totalTools
    → Call: sap.discovery.getNetworkOverview() or sap.registry.getGlobalRegistry()
    → Response: { totalAgents, activeAgents, totalTools, programId: 'SAPpUhsWLJG1FfkGRcXagEDMrMsWGjbky7AyhGpFETZ' }

  GET /v1/sap/agents?capability=payment:invoice
    → Query param: capability (required)
    → Returns: list of SAP agents with that capability (name, walletAddress, description, capabilityDetails)
    → Call: sap.discovery.findAgentsByCapability(capability) or findByCapability(capability)
    → Limit: max 20 results. Log: [sap-discovery] capability=X found=N

  GET /v1/sap/agents/me
    → Returns Invoica's own SAP profile: credentialData, capabilities, stats, agentPda
    → Uses process.env.SAP_AGENT_PDA to fetch the agent account
    → If SAP_AGENT_PDA not set → 503 { error: 'Invoica not yet registered on SAP' }
    → Call: sap.agent.get(new PublicKey(process.env.SAP_AGENT_PDA))
    → Or: sap.discovery.getAgentProfile(new PublicKey(process.env.SAP_AGENT_PDA))

  All routes:
    - Wrap in try/catch → 500 on SAP RPC error
    - Auth: require valid Invoica API key (use existing requireApiKey middleware)
    - Log all calls: [sap-routes] method=GET path=/v1/sap/...

WIRE INTO app.ts:
  Read backend/src/app.ts first to understand how routes are registered.
  Add: app.use('/v1/sap', requireApiKey, sapRouter);
  (or match existing routing pattern — could be /api/sap or similar)

FILE ADJUSTMENT NOTE:
  Check the actual method names available on the installed SapClient by reading
  node_modules/@oobe-protocol-labs/synapse-sap-sdk/dist/index.d.ts after SAP-001 installs deps.
  Map the correct discovery/agent method calls accordingly.

TEST: backend/src/routes/__tests__/sap.test.ts (NEW, max 80 lines)
  Mock getSapClient(). Test:
  - GET /v1/sap/network returns 200 with { totalAgents, programId }
  - GET /v1/sap/agents?capability=payment:invoice returns 200 with array
  - GET /v1/sap/agents without capability returns 400
  - GET /v1/sap/agents/me with SAP_AGENT_PDA unset returns 503
  - GET /v1/sap/agents/me with SAP_AGENT_PDA set returns 200

AFTER WRITING: npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -v node_modules — 0 new errors.

tasks.description: SAP x402 escrow bridge — detect SAP EscrowModule settlements on Solana and auto-create Invoica invoices
Draft @invoica_ai tweet thread announcing SAP integration — agent discovery, on-chain identity
tasks.context:
CMO SCOPE — READ FIRST: You are a CONTENT WRITING agent. Write the tweet thread as plain text in your response only. Do NOT create any files. Do NOT output file paths.

Context: Invoica just registered on Synapse Agent Protocol (SAP). SAP is a major on-chain agent identity/discovery layer on Solana. This is a meaningful distribution play — any of SAP's 100s of registered agents can now discover Invoica as a payment/invoicing service.

THREAD STRUCTURE (3-4 tweets, shipped-only, no roadmap):

Tweet 1 — The distribution angle:
  Invoica just registered on @OOBEProtocol SAP — the on-chain agent identity layer on Solana.
  Any agent using SAP discovery can now find Invoica and call our invoicing/settlement API.
  One API key. Four chains. Now discoverable on-chain.

Tweet 2 — What SAP is:
  SAP (Synapse Agent Protocol) gives AI agents verifiable on-chain identities.
  Capabilities, pricing, reputation — all on-chain. Permissionless discovery.
  Think: ENS but for agent services. With x402 payments baked in.

Tweet 3 — The technical detail:
  Invoica capabilities registered on SAP:
  → payment:invoice (x402, Base/Polygon/Arbitrum/Solana)
  → payment:settle
  → compliance:tax (12 countries)
  SAP Program: SAPpUhsWLJG1FfkGRcXagEDMrMsWGjbky7AyhGpFETZ
  Agent PDA: [insert SAP_AGENT_PDA from SAP-001 output]

Tweet 4 — CTA:
  Discover Invoica via SAP:
  client.discovery.findAgentsByCapability('payment:invoice')
  Or call directly: invoica.ai

RULES:
  - Only post AFTER SAP-001 registration confirmed on-chain
  - Include real Agent PDA from SAP-001 output in tweet 3
  - Each tweet max 280 chars
  - No emoji spam — max 1-2 per tweet
  - Technically precise tone
  - Tag @OOBEProtocol
